# Implementation Plan: VLM-Based ROI Content Recognition

**Branch**: `002-vlm-roi-recognition` | **Date**: 2025-12-29 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/002-vlm-roi-recognition/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan document generated by `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Integrate InternVL 3.5-1B vision-language model into the existing PDF document processing pipeline to automatically recognize content within extracted ROI regions (signatures, stamps, checkmarks, text fields). The system will process aligned documents, analyze predefined ROI regions using zero-shot VLM inference with Traditional Chinese prompts, and export structured CSV results with validation status. Key features include hardware-adaptive model loading (GPU/CPU with quantization), template-specific field schemas (contractor_1: 13 fields, contractor_2: 2 fields, enterprise_1: 14 fields), robust exception handling with automatic retry, and color-coded visualization overlays.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python 3.9+ (vlmcv environment)
**Primary Dependencies**:
- PyTorch 2.0+ (deep learning framework for VLM)
- Transformers 4.52.1+ (HuggingFace library for InternVL model)
- OpenCV 4.x (existing pipeline integration, already present)
- Pillow (PIL) (image format conversion for VLM)
- NumPy (array operations, already present)
- timm (vision transformer backbone, InternVL dependency)
- pandas (CSV export with proper encoding)

**Storage**: File-based storage
- Model cache: HuggingFace cache directory (~/.cache/huggingface/, ~2-4GB for InternVL 3.5-1B)
- CSV outputs: output/ directory
- Visualization images: output/ directory (existing pipeline enhanced with color-coded ROI boxes)

**Testing**: pytest (existing test framework)
- Unit tests with mocked VLM responses
- Integration tests with real InternVL model

**Target Platform**: Linux/Windows/macOS desktop environments
- GPU preferred (CUDA-capable NVIDIA GPU with 4GB+ VRAM)
- CPU fallback with INT8/INT4 quantization for systems with 8GB+ RAM

**Project Type**: Single project (Python library with CLI entry point via main.py)

**Performance Goals**:
- GPU inference: 0.1-0.5 seconds per ROI
- CPU inference (INT8): 1-3 seconds per ROI
- Batch processing: 100 documents in <10 minutes on mid-range GPU (RTX 3060)
- Single-page document on CPU: <30 seconds total

**Constraints**:
- VLM presence detection accuracy: ≥95% on clear fields, ≥85% on edge cases
- Text extraction success rate: ≥80% printed text, ≥60% handwritten text
- Zero existing functionality regression (all current features must work with VLM disabled)
- Must handle OOM errors gracefully with automatic retry/reload (max 3 retries)
- System must never crash or halt due to individual ROI failures

**Scale/Scope**:
- 3 document templates with distinct field counts
- 13-17 fields per document (including title, checkboxes, stamps, text, numbers)
- Support multi-page PDFs with sequential per-page processing
- Model size: ~2-4GB download on first run

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

**Status**: No constitution file present (template only found at `.specify/memory/constitution.md`). Proceeding with standard software engineering best practices:

✅ **Library-First Architecture**: VLM recognition implemented as modular library code in `vlm_pdf_recognizer/recognition/` that can be imported independently

✅ **Testability**: Unit tests with mocked VLM, integration tests with real model, clear separation of concerns

✅ **Backward Compatibility**: Existing pipeline preserved via optional VLM flag in main.py, zero regression requirement

✅ **Error Handling**: Robust exception handling with retry logic, graceful degradation, comprehensive logging

✅ **Performance**: Hardware-adaptive loading ensures deployment across GPU/CPU environments

**No violations detected** - Feature follows standard engineering practices for extending an existing Python library.

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
vlm_pdf_recognizer/
├── __init__.py
├── templates/                   # Existing: Template loading
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── template_cache.py
│   └── template_loader.py
├── preprocessing/               # Existing: PDF conversion, watermark removal
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── pdf_converter.py
│   └── watermark_removal.py
├── alignment/                   # Existing: Feature extraction, template matching
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── feature_extractor.py
│   ├── template_matcher.py
│   └── geometric_corrector.py
├── extraction/                  # Existing: ROI extraction
│   ├── __init__.py
│   └── roi_extractor.py
├── recognition/                 # NEW: VLM-based ROI content recognition
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── vlm_loader.py           # VLMLoader singleton, hardware detection, model loading
│   ├── field_schema.py         # FieldSchema, TemplateSchema, TEMPLATE_SCHEMAS mapping, prompt templates
│   ├── vlm_recognizer.py       # VLMRecognizer, RecognitionResult, DocumentRecognitionOutput
│   └── csv_exporter.py         # CSV export with UTF-8-sig, proper quoting
├── pipeline.py                  # MODIFIED: Integrate VLMRecognizer into existing pipeline
└── output.py                    # MODIFIED: Enhanced visualization with color-coded ROI boxes

tests/
├── unit/                        # NEW: Unit tests with mocked VLM
│   ├── test_vlm_loader.py
│   ├── test_field_schema.py
│   ├── test_vlm_recognizer.py
│   └── test_csv_exporter.py
└── integration/                 # NEW: Integration tests with real InternVL model
    ├── test_vlm_pipeline.py
    └── test_backward_compat.py

data/                            # Existing: Template definitions (unchanged)
input/                           # Existing: Input PDFs (unchanged)
output/                          # Existing: Output images and CSVs (new CSV format added)
main.py                          # MODIFIED: Add --enable-vlm flag, VLMLoader initialization
requirements.txt                 # MODIFIED: Add PyTorch, transformers, Pillow, timm, pandas
```

**Structure Decision**: Single project structure selected because this is an extension of the existing `vlm_pdf_recognizer` Python library. The new VLM recognition functionality is added as a new module (`vlm_pdf_recognizer/recognition/`) that integrates with the existing pipeline (`pipeline.py`). This maintains consistency with the current architecture while keeping the VLM feature isolated and independently testable.

## Phase 0: Research

**Status**: In progress (background research agent dispatched)

Research topics:
1. InternVL 3.5-1B integration pattern (model loading, image preprocessing, inference API)
2. Hardware detection & quantization strategy (CUDA detection, BitsAndBytes INT8/INT4, memory management)
3. OpenCV to PIL conversion for VLM input
4. Singleton pattern for model caching in Python
5. CSV export best practices with pandas (UTF-8-sig, Excel compatibility)

**Output**: [research.md](research.md) with technical decisions, rationales, alternatives, and code patterns

## Phase 1: Design

**Prerequisites**: research.md complete

### Data Model

**Output**: [data-model.md](data-model.md)

Key entities to define:
- **VLMConfig**: Hardware detection results, model precision, device type
- **FieldSchema**: Field name, field type (title/checkbox/stamp/text/number), prompt template, predefined value
- **TemplateSchema**: Template ID, ordered list of FieldSchema objects, validation rules
- **RecognitionResult**: Field ID, has_content (bool), content_text (string|null), raw_response, parse_success, inference_time_ms
- **DocumentRecognitionOutput**: Document metadata, page number, template ID, field results, processing timestamp, results validation status

State transitions:
- Model loading: Uninitialized → GPU Loading → GPU Loaded | CPU INT8 Loading → CPU INT8 Loaded | CPU INT4 Loading → CPU INT4 Loaded | Unquantized Loading → Unquantized Loaded | Failed
- ROI recognition: Pending → Processing → Success | Retry (up to 3 times) → Skipped

### API Contracts

**Output**: [contracts/](contracts/)

Internal Python API contracts (no REST/GraphQL endpoints - this is a library):

1. **VLMLoader API**:
   - `get_instance() → VLMLoader`: Singleton accessor
   - `load_model() → (model, tokenizer, device, precision)`: Hardware-adaptive model loading
   - `reload_model() → (model, tokenizer, device, precision)`: Force reload after OOM

2. **VLMRecognizer API**:
   - `__init__(model, tokenizer, template_schemas)`: Initialize with loaded VLM
   - `process_document(roi_images: List[np.ndarray], template_id: str, page_number: int, document_name: str) → DocumentRecognitionOutput`: Process all ROIs for a document

3. **CSV Exporter API**:
   - `export_recognition_results_to_csv(results: List[DocumentRecognitionOutput], output_path: str)`: Export to CSV with proper encoding

4. **Field Schema API**:
   - `TEMPLATE_SCHEMAS: Dict[str, TemplateSchema]`: Mapping of template IDs to schemas
   - `PROMPT_TEMPLATES: Dict[str, str]`: Traditional Chinese prompt templates for each field type

### Quickstart Guide

**Output**: [quickstart.md](quickstart.md)

Minimal example demonstrating VLM recognition on a single document:
- Environment setup (Python 3.9+, vlmcv environment)
- Dependencies installation (pip install -r requirements.txt)
- Model download (first run auto-downloads InternVL 3.5-1B)
- Processing a single PDF with VLM recognition enabled
- Interpreting CSV output and visualization images

## Implementation Notes

### Integration Points

1. **main.py**: Add optional `--enable-vlm` flag (default False for backward compatibility), initialize VLMLoader singleton, call VLMRecognizer after ROI extraction

2. **pipeline.py**: Modify `ProcessingResult` to include optional `recognition_output: DocumentRecognitionOutput`, add VLM recognition step after `extract_rois()`

3. **output.py**: Enhance `draw_roi_boxes()` to accept optional recognition results and draw color-coded boxes (green=has_content True, red=has_content False)

4. **requirements.txt**: Add PyTorch, transformers[torch], Pillow, timm, pandas with minimum version constraints

### Critical Dependencies

- **PyTorch Installation**: Must match CUDA version if GPU is available (users may need to manually install correct PyTorch version for their CUDA toolkit)

- **HuggingFace Model Download**: Requires internet access on first run (~2-4GB download), cached in ~/.cache/huggingface/ for subsequent runs

- **BitsAndBytes Compatibility**: INT8/INT4 quantization requires bitsandbytes library, which may have platform-specific installation issues on Windows (fallback to unquantized CPU mode if unavailable)

### Risk Mitigation

1. **Model Loading Failures**: Comprehensive error messages directing users to check internet connection, HuggingFace credentials, and disk space

2. **OOM Errors**: Automatic retry with lower quantization (GPU → CPU INT8 → CPU INT4 → unquantized with warning)

3. **VLM Inference Failures**: Retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff, skip ROI and continue processing if all retries fail

4. **Backward Compatibility**: Default VLM disabled in main.py, existing tests must pass without VLM module installed

## Success Criteria (from spec.md)

- **SC-001**: Batch processing of 100 documents in <10 minutes on mid-range GPU ✅ (addressed by GPU optimization in VLMLoader)

- **SC-002**: VLM presence detection ≥95% accuracy on clear fields, ≥85% on edge cases ✅ (addressed by field-specific Traditional Chinese prompts in FieldSchema)

- **SC-003**: Text extraction ≥80% printed, ≥60% handwritten ✅ (addressed by lenient content extraction with null fallback)

- **SC-004**: CPU processing <30 seconds per single-page document ✅ (addressed by INT8/INT4 quantization)

- **SC-005**: Zero existing functionality regression ✅ (addressed by optional VLM flag and isolated recognition module)

- **SC-006**: CSV files directly importable into Excel ✅ (addressed by UTF-8-sig encoding and QUOTE_NONNUMERIC in CSVExporter)
